title: Accessible progress bars with ETA, throttling, and motion-reduction
source: Terminal UX patterns from rich/tqdm conventions and prefers-reduced-motion accessibility principles (knowledge through Jan 2026)
description: Live spinners and progress indicators that repaint on every event flood non-TTY logs and can be disorienting. Throttling redraws to a fixed interval, showing an ETA/rate, and switching to a single static line (or plain status updates) when not attached to a TTY or when a RICK_REDUCE_MOTION flag is set improves both readability and accessibility while keeping the multi-Morty progress legible.
proposed_changes: Refactor progress.py to coalesce redraws behind a min-interval timer (e.g. 100ms) so rapid roro events don't thrash the terminal. Add ETA/throughput computation based on completed-vs-total ticket counts. Detect non-TTY and a RICK_REDUCE_MOTION env var to disable animated spinners, emitting discrete one-line status updates instead. Ensure the final state always prints a clean completed line even when animation is suppressed.
